43: 	EnvBoundarySRVLookup     = "BOUNDARY_SRV_LOOKUP"
44: )
45: 
46: // Config is used to configure the creation of the client
47: type Config struct {
48: 	// Addr is the address of the Boundary controller. This should be a
49: 	// complete URL such as "http://boundary.example.com". If you need a custom
50: 	// SSL cert or want to enable insecure mode, you need to specify a custom
51: 	// HttpClient.
52: 	Addr string
53: 
54: 	// Token is the client token that reuslts from authentication and can be
55: 	// used to make calls into Boundary
56: 	Token string
57: 
58: 	// RecoveryKmsWrapper is a wrapper used in the recovery KMS authentication
59: 	// flow. If set, this will always be used to generate a new token value
60: 	// per-call, regardless of any value set in Token.
61: 	RecoveryKmsWrapper wrapping.Wrapper
62: 
63: 	// HttpClient is the HTTP client to use. Boundary sets sane defaults for the
64: 	// http.Client and its associated http.Transport created in DefaultConfig.
65: 	// If you must modify Boundary's defaults, it is suggested that you start
66: 	// with that client and modify as needed rather than start with an empty
67: 	// client (or http.DefaultClient). Currently if the client is cloned the
68: 	// same HttpClient is used.
69: 	HttpClient *http.Client
70: 
71: 	// TLSConfig contains TLS configuration information. After modifying these
72: 	// values, ConfigureTLS should be called.
73: 	TLSConfig *TLSConfig
74: 
75: 	// Headers contains extra headers that will be added to any request
76: 	Headers http.Header
77: 
78: 	// MaxRetries controls the maximum number of times to retry when a 5xx
79: 	// error occurs. Set to 0 to disable retrying. Defaults to 2 (for a total
80: 	// of three tries).
81: 	MaxRetries int
82: 
83: 	// Timeout is for setting custom timeout parameter in the HttpClient
84: 	Timeout time.Duration
85: 
86: 	// The Backoff function to use; a default is used if not provided
87: 	Backoff retryablehttp.Backoff
88: 
89: 	// The CheckRetry function to use; a default is used if not provided
90: 	CheckRetry retryablehttp.CheckRetry
91: 
92: 	// Limiter is the rate limiter used by the client. If this pointer is nil,
93: 	// then there will be no limit set. In contrast, if this pointer is set,
94: 	// even to an empty struct, then that limiter will be used. Note that an
95: 	// empty Limiter is equivalent blocking all events. Currently if the client
96: 	// is cloned the same limiter is used.
97: 	Limiter *rate.Limiter
98: 
99: 	// OutputCurlString causes the actual request to return an error of type
100: 	// *OutputStringError. Type asserting the error message will allow
101: 	// fetching a cURL-compatible string for the operation.
102: 	OutputCurlString bool
103: 
104: 	// SRVLookup enables the client to lookup the host through DNS SRV lookup
105: 	SRVLookup bool
106: }
107: 
108: // TLSConfig contains the parameters needed to configure TLS on the HTTP client
109: // used to communicate with Boundary.
110: type TLSConfig struct {
111: 	// CACert is the path to a PEM-encoded CA cert file to use to verify the
112: 	// Boundary server SSL certificate.
113: 	CACert string
114: 
115: 	// CAPath is the path to a directory of PEM-encoded CA cert files to verify
116: 	// the Boundary server SSL certificate.
117: 	CAPath string
118: 
119: 	// ClientCert is the path to the certificate for Boundary communication
120: 	ClientCert string
121: 
122: 	// ClientKey is the path to the private key for Boundary communication
123: 	ClientKey string
124: 
125: 	// ServerName, if set, is used to set the SNI host when connecting via
126: 	// TLS.
127: 	ServerName string
128: 
129: 	// Insecure enables or disables SSL verification
130: 	Insecure bool
131: }
132: 
133: // DefaultConfig returns a default configuration for the client. It is
134: // safe to modify the return value of this function.
135: //
136: // The default Addr is http://127.0.0.1:9200, but this can be overridden by
137: // setting the `BOUNDARY_ADDR` environment variable.
138: //
139: // If an error is encountered, this will return nil.
140: func DefaultConfig() (*Config, error) {
141: 	config := &Config{
142: 		Addr:       "http://127.0.0.1:9200",
143: 		HttpClient: cleanhttp.DefaultPooledClient(),
144: 		Timeout:    time.Second * 60,
145: 		TLSConfig:  &TLSConfig{},
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	// We read the environment now; after DefaultClient returns we can override
149: 	// values from command line flags, which should take precedence.
150: 	if err := config.ReadEnvironment(); err != nil {
151: 		return config, fmt.Errorf("failed to read environment: %w", err)
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	transport := config.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport)
155: 	transport.TLSHandshakeTimeout = 10 * time.Second
156: 	transport.TLSClientConfig = &tls.Config{
157: 		MinVersion: tls.VersionTLS12,
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	config.Backoff = retryablehttp.LinearJitterBackoff
161: 	config.MaxRetries = 2
162: 	config.Headers = make(http.Header)
163: 
164: 	return config, nil
165: }
166: 
167: // ConfigureTLS takes a set of TLS configurations and applies those to the the
168: // HTTP client.
169: func (c *Config) ConfigureTLS() error {
170: 	if c.HttpClient == nil {
171: 		c.HttpClient = cleanhttp.DefaultPooledClient()
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	if c.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport).TLSClientConfig == nil {
175: 		c.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport).TLSClientConfig = &tls.Config{}
176: 	}
177: 	clientTLSConfig := c.HttpClient.Transport.(*http.Transport).TLSClientConfig
178: 
179: 	var clientCert tls.Certificate
180: 	foundClientCert := false
181: 
182: 	switch {
183: 	case c.TLSConfig.ClientCert != "" && c.TLSConfig.ClientKey != "":
184: 		var err error
185: 		clientCert, err = tls.LoadX509KeyPair(c.TLSConfig.ClientCert, c.TLSConfig.ClientKey)
186: 		if err != nil {
187: 			return err
188: 		}
189: 		foundClientCert = true
190: 	case c.TLSConfig.ClientCert != "" || c.TLSConfig.ClientKey != "":
191: 		return fmt.Errorf("both client cert and client key must be provided")
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	if c.TLSConfig.CACert != "" || c.TLSConfig.CAPath != "" {
195: 		rootConfig := &rootcerts.Config{
196: 			CAFile: c.TLSConfig.CACert,
197: 			CAPath: c.TLSConfig.CAPath,
198: 		}
199: 		if err := rootcerts.ConfigureTLS(clientTLSConfig, rootConfig); err != nil {
